Title: Negative Regulation of Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in Arabidopsis by a miR156-Targeted SPL Transcription Factor

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary metabolites involved in plant defense and pigmentation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased miR156 activity promotes anthocyanin accumulation and reduces flavonol accumulation, whereas reduced miR156 activity has the opposite effect through regulation of SPL genes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pro35S:MIR156 overexpression; Pro35S:MIM156 target mimicry; anthocyanin quantification; LC-MS flavonol profiling; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern: declines as the plant progresses during development; high miR156 inferred where anthocyanin accumulates strongly
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of miR156 caused ~4-fold higher stem anthocyanin accumulation. 2. Reduced miR156 activity in Pro35S:MIM156 strongly reduced anthocyanin and increased kaempferol derivatives. 3. miR156 regulates flavonoid flux indirectly by repressing SPL transcription factors.
Common_Genes.Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in Pro35S:MIR156 and Pro35S:MIM156
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced miR156 activity shifts metabolism toward flavonols and away from anthocyanins; developmental phase-change effects are expected because miR156-SPL controls flowering and phase transition
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a protein-coding gene; functions upstream of SPL family members targeted by miR156.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NADPH-dependent oxidoreductase of the short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase-related dihydroflavonol 4-reductase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins using NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher DFR expression is associated with increased anthocyanin accumulation and reduced flux to kaempferol-type flavonols; SPL9 represses DFR to suppress anthocyanin production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ProDFR:GUS reporter; DEX-inducible ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR with cycloheximide; ChIP-qPCR showing SPL9 recruitment to DFR promoter
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: highest in basal stem region and lowest in apical stem; promoter active at stem-rosette junction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch-control step supported by flux competition with FLS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR transcript increased >30-fold in Pro35S:MIR156 and decreased in Pro35S:MIM156. 2. DFR expression pattern inversely correlated with SPL9/SPL15 along the stem. 3. SPL9 directly represses DFR-associated promoter activity and thereby reduces anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: long-day growth; DEX plus CHX induction in ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: native DFR promoter used in ProDFR:GUS and mutated GTAC-box reporter
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SPL9; PAP1; TT8; TTG1 complex acts on promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful branch-point target to increase anthocyanins, but flux tradeoff with flavonols should be considered; stacking with upstream activators or blocking SPL9 repression may enhance pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing DFR-driven anthocyanin flux may reduce flavonol accumulation due to substrate competition with FLS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SPL9 is recruited to the DFR promoter, mutation of GTAC motifs did not alter promoter pattern, suggesting indirect recruitment via protein complex interactions.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts dihydroflavonols to flavonols via desaturation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FLS1 expression was not greatly changed by altered SPL activity, but FLS1 competes with DFR for common substrates and therefore contributes to increased flavonol accumulation when anthocyanin branch is repressed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; LC-MS flavonol profiling; DEX-inducible SPL9 assay showing FLS1 transcript not significantly affected
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: branch-competition enzyme; not established here as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLS1 is the major FLS gene in Arabidopsis referenced in this study. 2. Its expression changed little in Pro35S:MIR156 or Pro35S:MIM156. 3. Flavonol output still rose when SPL activity was high, consistent with reduced DFR-mediated competition rather than direct FLS1 activation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: long-day growth; DEX plus CHX induction in ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be paired with DFR manipulation to tune anthocyanin/flavonol ratio rather than total flavonoid flux.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced flavonol branch may reduce anthocyanin pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes flux competition between FLS and DFR rather than direct SPL9-mediated transcriptional regulation of FLS1.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SBP/SPL) family; DNA-binding SBP domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: branch selection in flavonoid biosynthesis; repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression via destabilization of a MYB-bHLH-WD40 activation complex and indirect recruitment to target promoter
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses expression of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, especially DFR; indirectly shifts flux toward flavonol formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SPL9 negatively regulates anthocyanin accumulation and promotes a shift toward flavonol accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression analysis; inducible rSPL9-GR repression assay with DEX/CHX; ChIP-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase BiFC; coimmunoprecipitation; transient competition assay in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: higher in apical stem and lower in basal stem, inverse to DFR expression; rises during development as miR156 declines
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: possible indirect feedback through flavonoid pathway regulators is discussed, but not established for SPL9
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: acts by interfering with the PAP1/TT8/TTG1 MYB-bHLH-WD40 activation complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major negative regulator linking developmental transition to anthocyanin suppression and flavonol-promoting flux redistribution
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: long-day growth at 23°C; DEX treatment in ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR seedlings; transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: native SPL9 regulatory sequences in ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR and ProSPL9:GFP-rSPL9; 35S constitutive promoter in overexpression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing SPL9 or weakening its interaction with PAP1 could elevate anthocyanin while preserving developmental timing traits must be considered; useful target for tuning anthocyanin/flavonol ratio.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because SPL9 also regulates phase change, flowering, trichome initiation, embryonic development, cell size, and fertility, manipulating it may cause broad developmental pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPL9 overexpression decreased anthocyanin accumulation, while reduced SPL activity via miR156 increased anthocyanin. 2. SPL9 directly associates with the DFR promoter and rapidly represses DFR transcription. 3. SPL9 binds PAP1 and competes with TT8, destabilizing the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex to inhibit anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation of GTAC motifs in the DFR promoter did not alter reporter pattern, indicating that SPL9 likely acts through protein-protein interaction-mediated recruitment rather than direct canonical DNA binding at those sites.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation as part of the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR and other anthocyanin biosynthetic genes as complex component
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates anthocyanin pathway genes together with TT8 and TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PAP1 activity is required for anthocyanin accumulation and for the elevated anthocyanin phenotype caused by miR156 overexpression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pap1-migs knockdown in Pro35S:MIR156 background; yeast two-hybrid with SPL9 and TT8; luciferase BiFC; coimmunoprecipitation; transient interaction competition assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: moderately increased in Pro35S:MIR156 and not dramatically changed in Pro35S:MIM156 stems
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: SPL9 interference at protein level; possible positive feedback discussed
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: positive feedback on PAP1 expression is suggested but not proven
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: core MYB subunit of the PAP1/TT8/TTG1 MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential activator required for anthocyanin accumulation and target of SPL9-mediated complex destabilization
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: long-day growth; transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: 35S promoter in transient assays and PAP1-MIGS construct context
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase BiFC; coIP
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong activator for anthocyanin biofortification, but benefits depend on overcoming SPL9/MYBL2-mediated repression and balancing pathway flux.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced anthocyanin activation may alter flux away from flavonols and other phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAP1 is necessary for Pro35S:MIR156-induced anthocyanin accumulation. 2. PAP1 directly interacts with both TT8 and SPL9. 3. SPL9 competes with TT8 for PAP1 binding, weakening the activation complex and repressing anthocyanin genes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A true pap1 mutant in Col-0 was unavailable, so PAP1 function was tested using MIGS knockdown.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation as part of the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR and other anthocyanin biosynthetic genes as complex component
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression with PAP1/PAP2-type MYBs and TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TT8 contributes to anthocyanin accumulation and is part of the activation complex whose assembly is disrupted by SPL9.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: tt8-1 mutant in Pro35S:MIR156 background; yeast two-hybrid with PAP1; transient luciferase complementation and SPL9 competition assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: moderately increased in Pro35S:MIR156 and not dramatically changed in Pro35S:MIM156 stems
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: complex formation with PAP1 and TTG1; antagonized by SPL9
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: TT8 expression is described as being controlled by a positive feedback loop in discussion
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: core bHLH subunit of the PAP1/TT8/TTG1 MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PAP1; TTG1; antagonized by SPL9 competition for PAP1 binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important but partially redundant activator of anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: long-day growth; transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can enhance anthocyanin output when combined with compatible MYB/WD40 partners, but redundancy with GL3/EGL3 means single-gene manipulation may be insufficient.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because of redundancy and broader epidermal regulatory roles, effects may be context-dependent
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tt8 mutation reduced the elevated anthocyanin phenotype of Pro35S:MIR156 plants. 2. TT8 directly binds PAP1. 3. SPL9 suppresses PAP1-TT8 interaction, destabilizing the activation complex.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TT8 is functionally redundant with GL3 and EGL3, so loss of TT8 alone only partially suppressed anthocyanin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression as scaffold/cofactor
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR and other anthocyanin biosynthetic genes indirectly through complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: required for activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes by MYB-bHLH partners
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TTG1 activity is required for anthocyanin accumulation and for the high-anthocyanin phenotype in Pro35S:MIR156 plants.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ttg1-1 suppression of Pro35S:MIR156 anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: complex assembly with PAP1/PAP2-type MYBs and TT8/GL3/EGL3 bHLHs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: obligate WD40 component of the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential structural cofactor for anthocyanin-promoting transcriptional complex
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary partner for anthocyanin activation modules, though pleiotropic roles in trichome biology may complicate deployment.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss or strong alteration affects both anthocyanin accumulation and trichome initiation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ttg1-1 completely suppressed anthocyanin accumulation in Pro35S:MIR156 inflorescences. 2. TTG1 is required for the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex that activates DFR and related genes. 3. The SPL9 effect depends on an intact TTG1-containing activation complex.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TTG1 was not shown to interact directly with SPL9 in this paper but is genetically required for the SPL-mediated phenotype.
